mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and raise corporate borrowing costs, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which contracts corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening economic output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansions during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with a win rate below 45% or a negative cumulative alpha over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue streams, which systematically supports higher equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 330 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches 330, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy, with forward returns showing a negative mean or a Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal renewed confidence in long-term economic stability and construction sector demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Housing starts increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a win rate below 40% or a statistically insignificant predictive coefficient (p > 0.15).
